Entity_Description: Chilliwack is a city of 93,203 (2021 Census) in British Columbia's Fraser Valley Regional District, about 86 km east of Vancouver along Highway 1. It sits on the fertile Fraser River floodplain beneath Mount Cheam, where the Chilliwack River becomes the Vedder River at Vedder Crossing. First incorporated as a township in 1873, it is one of BC's oldest municipalities and an agricultural heartland known for dairy and corn. Housing ranges from heritage farmhouses to post-2000 subdivisions such as Garrison Crossing, built on former CFB Chilliwack lands, and hillside homes on Promontory.

Long_Description:
Stand almost anywhere in Chilliwack and Mount Cheam dominates the skyline — a daily reminder that this city of 93,203 lives on a broad Fraser River floodplain framed by the Cascades. That geography shapes everything about owning property here, and it shapes how {COMPANY_NAME} delivers {SERVICE} across the community.

Chilliwack is really several places stitched together. North of the highway, the historic downtown around Five Corners carries housing that dates back generations, reflecting a municipality first incorporated in 1873. South of the Vedder River lies Sardis, just 3 km away and long the city's growth engine. Garrison Crossing, redeveloped from the former CFB Chilliwack base lands, mixes converted military-era buildings with newer townhomes, while Promontory climbs the hillside with steep driveways and post-2000 construction. Push further out and you reach Yarrow, Vedder Crossing, and the rural acreages that make this BC's agricultural heartland — dairy barns, poultry operations, and the sweet-corn fields Chilliwack is famous for, many on well and septic rather than city services.

Each of these settings asks something different of a {BUSINESS_TYPE}. The floodplain neighbourhoods deal with heavy winter rain and high groundwater; nobody here has forgotten November 2021, when atmospheric-river flooding across the Sumas Prairie cut Highway 1 and made valley drainage everyone's problem. Chilliwack also sees more temperature swing than Metro Vancouver — outflow winds can bring hard freezes and snow, then summers turn genuinely hot. Properties that ride out both extremes need {SERVICE} planned by people who understand the pattern.

The economy adds its own demands. Alongside farming, Chilliwack has drawn manufacturing and steady residential growth along Highway 1, which means a constant pipeline of new builds sitting next to homes from an earlier century. {COMPANY_NAME} serves that full spectrum: {SERVICE_LIST}, delivered for every {PROPERTY_TYPE} from a downtown heritage home to a Promontory hillside build to a working farm.

What you get with {COMPANY_NAME} is a team that reads Chilliwack's terrain correctly the first time — {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP} — and shows up when promised, with response times of {RESPONSE_TIME} and a {ESTIMATE_TYPE} before work begins. From Five Corners to Cultus Lake's doorstep, {CTA_PRIMARY} — call {PHONE} or visit {WEBSITE} to get started.
Local_Facts:
- Population 93,203 (2021 Census), one of the largest cities in the Fraser Valley Regional District
- Located about 86 km east of Vancouver on Highway 1, on the Fraser River floodplain beneath Mount Cheam
- First incorporated as a township in 1873, making it one of British Columbia's oldest municipalities
- The Chilliwack River becomes the Vedder River at Vedder Crossing, about 7 km from the city centre
- Garrison Crossing neighbourhood was redeveloped from the former CFB Chilliwack military base lands
- Part of BC's agricultural heartland, known for dairy, poultry, and Chilliwack sweet corn
- The November 2021 atmospheric-river flooding of the nearby Sumas Prairie closed Highway 1 and made valley drainage a local priority
